texto: Aside from the inescapable realities of birth and death, we’re responsible for the paths our lives take. We each make our own choices.
explicacao: Citação de Alissa Johnson sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Aside from the inescapable realities of birth and death, we’re responsible for the paths our live…” articula vida, choice, path em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como luto e vida.

frase_original: Aside from the inescapable realities of birth and death, we’re responsible for the paths our lives take. We each make our own choices.

texto: How had things gone so terribly wrong? She wasn't supposed to be returning to Murdoch House in defeat, and she most certainly was not supposed to be returning alone.Lilly should be there. And Gideon. High-handed, muleheaded, wonderful Gideon. She'd never admitted it, not even to herself, but a part of her had expected him to come back to Murdoch House with her. Or perhaps it was more accurate to say that no part of her had been able to imagine going back without him.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Alissa Johnson que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “How had things gone so terribly wrong? She wasn't supposed to be returning to Murdoch House in de…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, historical-romance, supposed, com foco em reflexao.

She wasn't supposed to be returning to Murdoch House in defeat, and she most certainly was not supposed to be returning alone.Lilly should be there. And Gideon. High-handed, muleheaded, wonderful Gideon. She'd never admitted it, not even to herself, but a part of her had expected him to come back to Murdoch House with her. Or perhaps it was more accurate to say that no part of her had been able to imagine going back without him.
